description: Phase 5 vision-critic loop. Each round a Sonnet vision agent scored a render of the anchored layout against readability criteria (Purchase/Ware/Huang/Gestalt), then concrete fixes were applied and the graph re-rendered. Screenshots: critic_r{1..6}_{full,zoom}.png, clean_edges_off.png. The critic is genuinely strict — it never rubber-stamped and plateaued the layout at ~6/10.

applied_next: route cross-faction edges via perimeter gateways (break starburst)
stop truncating faction labels + add gaps
fan each edge's gateway by source position

rounds.note: convergence at Ebon Strongbear identified as a genuine high-degree hub, not a layout bug
applied_next: AGGREGATE cross-faction NPC-NPC edges into one ribbon per faction pair (kill the hairball)
bow ribbons outward into peripheral corridors

rounds.note: BEST version. Aggregation + outward corridors removed the hairball.
applied_next: obstacle-avoidance: push ribbon control points away from intervening blobs

rounds.note: REGRESSION — obstacle avoidance traded blob-slicing for ribbon-on-ribbon crossings + labels cut by ribbons. A Pareto trade, not a win. Reverted to round-5 routing.
Even with NO edges, plateaus at 6 — the limiter is density imbalance inherent to the faithful spawn geography (dense guild district vs empty outskirts).

conclusion: The loop works and is honestly critical. Aggregation was the real win (hairball -> ~6/10). Past that it plateaus: (1) obstacle-avoidance is a Pareto trade (fixes slicing, adds crossings) — the paper's multi-objective tension, live; (2) the remaining ceiling is whitespace/density imbalance that is INHERENT to anchoring on real spawn geography (faithfulness vs readability). Reaching 7-8 would require abandoning strict geography (dial the lambda slider toward 'emergent'), density-equalizing distortion (cartogram), or research-grade learned/joint layout. The critic correctly diagnosed this rather than chasing an unfixable artifact.
